tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. Urine is cloudy, brown, and contains glucose, ketones, and bilirubin, raising concern for renal and metabolic dysfunction. HbA1c is low, possibly reflecting anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology and metabolic disturbances.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
